title: 7 Micro Habits to Escape the Freeze Response - Polyvagal Tools for Depression & Burnout
claim: The freeze response or shutdown state, characterized by sluggishness, exhaustion, burnout, or depression, is a common reaction to trauma, chronic stress, or overwhelming situations, including prolonged negative thought patterns.

title: 7 Micro Habits to Escape the Freeze Response - Polyvagal Tools for Depression & Burnout
claim: The nervous system operates in three main states according to the Polyvagal theory: Ventral Vagal (safe, connected, flexible in energy and rest), Sympathetic (anxious, activated, fearful), and Dorsal Vagal (overwhelmed shutdown, mentally and physically).

title: 7 Micro Habits to Escape the Freeze Response - Polyvagal Tools for Depression & Burnout
claim: The dorsal vagal shutdown state manifests with symptoms like depression, burnout, hopelessness, and unmotivation, and is associated with physiological changes such as slowed heart rate, dropped blood pressure, and altered appetite, leading to feelings of heaviness.

title: 7 Micro Habits to Escape the Freeze Response - Polyvagal Tools for Depression & Burnout
claim: Physical changes and lack of energy experienced in the dorsal vagal state are not the individual's fault, and there are actionable steps that can be taken to address and overcome them.

title: 7 Micro Habits to Escape the Freeze Response - Polyvagal Tools for Depression & Burnout
claim: To regulate the nervous system and move out of freeze, it's crucial to help it identify cues of safety in the environment, a process known as neuroception, which can be practiced by slowly scanning and noticing five visual safety indicators.

title: 7 Micro Habits to Escape the Freeze Response - Polyvagal Tools for Depression & Burnout
claim: Movement is one of the most powerful ways to get unstuck from the freeze response because it signals to the nervous system, at a biological level, that the individual is not in danger and possesses the capacity to act.

title: 7 Micro Habits to Escape the Freeze Response - Polyvagal Tools for Depression & Burnout
claim: Simple movements, such as stretching, wiggling fingers and toes, or taking a quick walk, are effective micro-habits to activate the nervous system and initiate a shift out of the freeze state.

title: 7 Micro Habits to Escape the Freeze Response - Polyvagal Tools for Depression & Burnout
claim: Experimenting with temperature, through methods like washing the face with cold water or gradually changing shower temperatures, can help the nervous system get unstuck from freeze by triggering positive effects like slowing heart rate and expanding one's window of tolerance.

title: 7 Micro Habits to Escape the Freeze Response - Polyvagal Tools for Depression & Burnout
claim: Often, transitioning from the dorsal vagal shutdown response to a safe and connected ventral vagal state requires an intermediate phase of nervous system activation.

title: 7 Micro Habits to Escape the Freeze Response - Polyvagal Tools for Depression & Burnout
claim: Activating the vagus nerve, which is deeply involved in the connection between the mind and body, can help to calm and regulate the nervous system.

title: 7 Micro Habits to Escape the Freeze Response - Polyvagal Tools for Depression & Burnout
claim: Humming, singing a tune, or letting out a long sigh effectively stimulates the vagus nerve through vibration, offering an energy-efficient method to regulate the nervous system without demanding significant effort.

title: 7 Micro Habits to Escape the Freeze Response - Polyvagal Tools for Depression & Burnout
claim: Gently massaging or tapping areas of the face (e.g., under eyes, cheeks, forehead) can stimulate the vagus nerve, which runs through the face, sending signals of safety to the nervous system.

title: 7 Micro Habits to Escape the Freeze Response - Polyvagal Tools for Depression & Burnout
claim: Prioritizing "real rest" is essential for the nervous system to truly recharge and restore itself, contrasting with screen time which often only distracts while keeping the nervous system in a state of stress.

title: 7 Micro Habits to Escape the Freeze Response - Polyvagal Tools for Depression & Burnout
claim: Many individuals experience burnout and exhaustion because they mistake passive screen consumption for genuine rest, metaphorically carrying their stress while their nervous system remains un-recharged.

title: 7 Micro Habits to Escape the Freeze Response - Polyvagal Tools for Depression & Burnout

title: 7 Micro Habits to Escape the Freeze Response - Polyvagal Tools for Depression & Burnout
